name: Jim McNeely
abstract: Jim McNeely (May 18, 1949 – September 26, 2025) was an American jazz pianist, composer, arranger and academic.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: get people like Jim McNeely, so many good arrangers, to take standard repertoire and re-write it in a contemporary way. The best way I can describe it is I told Jim McNeely, 'Write Sing, Sing, Sing, your own version of “Sing, Sing, Sing.”'
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: Jim McNeely who was one of my pianist at one point... piano players, it was first was Mike Mellilo, then it was Hal Galper, then Jim McNeely, and then Bill Charlap.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: Jim McNeely and I are the music directors at the BMI Jazz Composers Workshop.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: So that became like a little signature that I did that Roland Hanna before me, and nobody did, and Jim McNeely after me didn’t do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: This is what Jim McNeely was doing with Jon Faddis and that orchestra...
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: this music wouldn’t exist if somebody here in the West German radio wouldn’t take money and say to ... Jim McNeely ... say please write a program ... being part of this creation is quite something.
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023
